title: Edge Hub Local Storage and SQLite Backlog
spl:
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub
| stats latest(disk_usage) as disk_pct, latest(backlog_count) as backlog by host
| where disk_pct > 85 OR backlog > 2000000
| table host disk_pct backlog _time
description: Edge Hub stores backlog in SQLite when disconnected. Disk usage and backlog size must stay within limits (e.g. 3M sensor points) to avoid data loss and device lockup.
value: Edge Hub stores backlog in SQLite when disconnected. Disk usage and backlog size must stay within limits (e.g. 3M sensor points) to avoid data loss and device lockup.
implementation: Edge Hub reports disk and backlog to edge-hub-health. Alert when disk exceeds 85% or backlog approaches device limit (e.g. 2M). Plan connectivity and storage upgrades before saturation.
visualization: Gauge (disk %), Line chart (backlog over time), Table (devices near limit).
knownFalsePositives: SQLite backlog during offline buffering, site power events, or disk cleanup on the edge host.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (device health).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-health` (disk_usage, backlog_count).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Edge Hub reports disk and backlog to edge-hub-health. Alert when disk exceeds 85% or backlog approaches device limit (e.g. 2M). Plan connectivity and storage upgrades before saturation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edge-hub-health sourcetype=edge_hub
| stats latest(disk_usage) as disk_pct, latest(backlog_count) as backlog by host
| where disk_pct > 85 OR backlog > 2000000
| table host disk_pct backlog _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Edge Hub Local Storage and SQLite Backlog** — Edge Hub stores backlog in SQLite when disconnected. Disk usage and backlog size must stay within limits (e.g. 3M sensor points) to avoid data loss and device lockup.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-health` (disk_usage, backlog_count). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (device health). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-health; **sourcetype**: edge_hub. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edge-hub-health, sourcetype=edge_hub. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where disk_pct > 85 OR backlog > 2000000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Edge Hub Local Storage and SQLite Backlog**): table host disk_pct backlog _time

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a health or backlog window you understand, then compare with the Splunk Edge Hub admin views and local health on the edge host. Reconcile queue, backlog, and service fields line by line.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (disk %), Line chart (backlog over time), Table (devices near limit).
grandmaExplanation: We watch local storage and backlogs on the edge box so a full disk or stuck queue does not block messaging into our platform.
